description: Productize the current smoke-driven WeChat Claw slice into a directly deliverable single-machine internal MVP with a real runtime entrypoint, classified startup diagnostics, restart-safe approval recovery, handoff-grade docs, and refreshed verification coverage.

userStories.title: Add a real long-running runtime entrypoint
userStories.description: As an internal operator, I want a real `start:wechat` runtime entrypoint so that I can launch the WeChat/TUI runtime directly instead of routing through the smoke verifier.
acceptanceCriteria: The runtime bootstraps the app, awaits `runtime.start()`, and then launches the TUI
`package.json` exposes `npm run start:wechat` that builds and runs `dist/main-entrypoint.js`

userStories.title: Classify startup failures with actionable diagnostics
userStories.description: As an operator, I want startup failures categorized by source so that missing config, provider issues, database problems, and gateway issues fail clearly instead of crashing opaquely.
acceptanceCriteria: `describeConfigError(...)` summarizes missing required config in stable order
`bootstrapApplication(...)` rethrows typed startup failures with categories including `config`, `provider`, `database`, and `gateway`
Both smoke and real runtime entrypoints print readable startup failure messages without noisy stacks

userStories.title: Keep pending approvals actionable after restart
userStories.description: As an operator, I want pending approvals to survive restart and still be actionable from the real runtime path so that restarts do not lose work in progress.
acceptanceCriteria: A persisted pending approval remains visible to the task service after `runMainEntrypoint(...)` restarts the runtime
The TUI still selects the recovered approval thread and can approve the recovered approval from a restart-shaped snapshot
Recovery behavior is verified in `tests/app/entrypoint.test.ts` and `tests/tui/runtime.test.ts`

userStories.title: Ship deliverable docs and configuration artifacts
userStories.description: As an internal teammate, I want `.env.example` and the README to describe the smoke command, real runtime command, recovery expectations, and troubleshooting steps so that I can install and operate the system from docs alone.
acceptanceCriteria: `.env.example` contains exactly the currently supported runtime variables
`README.md` documents install, configure, smoke verification, real runtime startup, restart/recovery expectations, and troubleshooting
`docs/plans/README.md` points to the active deliverable-MVP design and implementation docs
Focused doc/runtime verification passes and both runtime commands fail clearly on missing config

userStories.title: Refresh verification and Ralph tracking for the deliverable MVP slice
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want focused verification, full repository verification, runtime contract checks, and updated Ralph tracking so the branch state matches the new deliverable MVP runtime contract.
acceptanceCriteria: Focused verification passes for entrypoint, bootstrap, config, TUI, and smoke coverage
`npm test`, `npx tsc --noEmit`, and `npm run build` all pass
`npm run start:mvp` remains the smoke verifier and `npm run start:wechat` remains the real runtime path
`ralph/prd.json` and `ralph/progress.txt` describe the deliverable internal MVP slice and verification evidence
